summary_one_line: Input must carry a well-formed 'post' table whose name index resolves a glyph, requiring both the 'post' FOURCC tag and a matching name entry.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The literal 4-byte table tag 'post' (70 6f 73 74) placed at the table-directory offset (here 0x0c-0x0f), so the OpenType loader recognizes and parses a post table whose name lookup can succeed.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds contain the exact ASCII run 'post' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (70 6f 73 74) plus the 'true' magic at 0x00-0x03, and value_profile_cmplog resolves the true branch in 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds carry no 'post' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (they show 'kern'/'fff'/'erdn' style bytes there) and resolve the branch 0/10, because plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace to substitute the exact 'post' FOURCC into the directory offset.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the table-tag comparison against the literal 'post', and substitutes that operand into the seed at the directory offset; value_profile has no such substitution stage so it cannot place the exact 4-byte tag.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Beyond the 'post' tag, the input must supply post-table substructure (version/name-index/string bytes) close enough to a valid name entry that get_glyph_from_name returns a glyph; partial-match feedback is needed to incrementally home in on it.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog carries CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward inputs whose post-table CMP operands move closer to matching, and it resolves the branch 8/10 with avg hitcount 4 on the branch.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain cmplog has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP partial-match feedback, so it cannot preserve incremental progress toward a valid name entry and resolves only 1/10 trials (blocked 9).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) preserves corpus inputs that get closer to a satisfying post-table name comparison; cmplog lacks this feedback so partial progress is discarded between executions.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a post-table glyph-name lookup that must succeed to take the true branch.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the literal 'post' tag and 'true' magic.
At the table-directory offsets 0x0c-0x0f the winner bytes are 'post' while loser bytes differ.
value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile via the I2S delta, resolving 8/10 vs 0/10.
value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog via the value_profile delta, resolving 8/10 vs 1/10.
The winner attains a nonzero hitcount on the branch while losers stay at zero.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=4  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a matched CMP operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP preserves inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching as new corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog unions the CMP_MAP gradient with the cmplog buffer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5442 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 6 --seed 39fb20c9e671238a` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 26 of the 27-level ancestor chain; fuzzer is vpc (not mopt/grimoire) so the dash signal is I2S-exclusive under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the true branch on a 'post' FOURCC tag in which value_profile resolves the branch as often as value_profile_cmplog (i.e. I2S substitution gives no advantage in placing the exact 4-byte tag).

weakest_evidence_point: The Pair-2 claim that CMP_MAP gradient (not just I2S) is independently necessary rests on the cmplog loser's 1/10 resolution being attributable to missing partial-match feedback rather than scheduling variance.
